title: trichocline eriopus
intro: Trichocline eriopus, also known as New Zealand hawkweed, is a plant species native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Trichocline eriopus can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
